     The Numbers Business Report

Capote (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $28,750,530Details
International Box Office $21,173,549
Worldwide Box Office $49,924,079
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,059,169
Total $17,059,169
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Reading of the murder of a Kansas family, New York City novelist Truman Capote decides to cover the story himself, and travels to the small town with his childhood friend, aspiring novelist Harper Lee. When Perry Smith and Dick Hickock are arrested and charged, Capote forms an emotional bond with Smith during his jailhouse interviews despite the young criminal's apparent guilt.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$324,857 (1.1% of total gross)
Legs:12.55 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:57.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$7,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:12 opening theaters/1,239 max. theaters, 6.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $52,972,658

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 30th, 2005 (Limited) by Sony Pictures Classics
Video Release: March 21st, 2006 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for some violent images and brief strong language.
Running Time: 114 minutes
Award nomination: 2006 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Geographical setting: Kansas
Plot point: Addiction, Biography, LGBTQ+, Writing and Writers
Social setting: Death Row
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1950s, 1960s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: United Artists, Sony Pictures Classics, A-Line Pictures, Coopers Town Productions, Infinity Media
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

Reading of the murder of a Kansas family, New York City novelist Truman Capote decides to cover the story himself, and travels to the small town with his childhood friend, aspiring novelist Harper Lee. When Perry Smith and Dick Hickock are arrested and charged, Capote forms an emotional bond with Smith during his jailhouse interviews despite the young criminal's apparent guilt.
